zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Turberville於1235年出生在英國威爾斯格拉莫爾根可迪時，他的父親Gilbert de Turberville35歲，母親Meiwen Cadwgan31歲。他於1255年與Agnes Wilcock在英國威爾斯格拉莫爾根結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1283年在他的家鄉享年48歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Turberville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name, probably from Thouberville (Eure), or possibly from Trubleville (Seine-Maritime), both in France. The two may have been confused in surname usage.

variants.name: Torbet
meanings: from the Anglo-Norman and Middle English personal name T(h)urbert, T(h)orbert, a hybrid of Old Norse Thorr ‘Thor’, the name of the god of thunder, + ancient Germanic -berht ‘bright, famous’. The name was probably formed on the Continent; see Turberville .
perhaps in some cases a nickname from Old French and Middle English turbot, turbut ‘turbot’, for someone who caught or sold or resembled this fish.

variants.name: Tuberville
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Turberville  . This form of the surname is very rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Turville
meanings: English (Surrey and Hampshire; of Norman origin):
habitational name from Tourville‐la‐Campagne (Eure, France). The placename derives from the Old Norse personal name Thórr + Old French ville ‘farmstead, estate’ (from Latin villa).
occasionally a shortened form of   Turberville  .

variants.name: Troublefield
meanings: Probably an altered form of   Turberville  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Turberville was born in 1235, in Coity, Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom, his father, Gilbert de Turberville, was 35 and his mother, Meiwen Cadwgan, was 31. He married Agnes Wilcock in 1255, in Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died in 1283, in his hometown, at the age of 48.

Il a épousé Agnes Wilcock en 1255, à Glamorgan, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé en 1283, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 48 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Turberville(이)가 1235년 영국 웨일스 글램르건 코이티에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gilbert de Turberville(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Meiwen Cadwgan(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1255년 영국 웨일스 글램르건에서 Agnes Wilcock(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1283년 그의 고향에서 48세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Turberville родился в 1235 г. в местности коити, глэморган, Уэльс, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Gilbert de Turberville было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Meiwen Cadwgan, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Wilcock в 1255 г. в местности глэморган, Уэльс, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер в 1283 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 48 лет.
